steps.title: Identify what fits across the blank
steps.content:
The sentence reads: "exhibited her work for audiences across the ___________ vivid pastel colors, overexposed tones, and mirrorlike symmetry to evoke a Socialist-era aesthetic." The blank comes after "across the" and before "vivid pastel colors." The word "utilizing" after the blank introduces a participial phrase describing how she made her photos. The blank must complete the object of "across the" (a noun for where she exhibited) and then allow "utilizing" to start a new grammatical unit.
  • Choice A — 'world utilizing': No punctuation between "world" and "utilizing" fuses the two clauses into a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'world and utilizing': The conjunction "and" tries to coordinate "utilizing" with no clear parallel element, producing an incoherent structure. ✗
  • Choice C — 'world, utilizing': A comma alone between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — 'world. Utilizing': A period ends the first independent clause; "Utilizing" then begins the second sentence as a participial phrase modifying the subject. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the sentence boundary
steps.content: With choice D, the first sentence ends at "world." The second sentence begins "Utilizing vivid pastel colors, overexposed tones, and mirrorlike symmetry to evoke a Socialist-era aesthetic, her photos captivated viewers from South Korea to Portugal." This is a complete, well-formed sentence with a participial opener followed by the subject "her photos" and the verb "captivated." No other choice produces two grammatically correct sentences. That matches choice D — D is correct.
